behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior for recursive/nested paths (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). A mocked watcher can report nested events even if real watcher config/OS behavior misses them, so Integration can falsely pass. Rejected lower level due to behavior text: "placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root" (requires real watch semantics).
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree. Create a nested subdirectory and write a file there. Assert the transformation runs and output is produced, without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is scoped to deterministic dedup key/time-window logic for one path (MOCK-MISS says single-function/object logic). No real OS/network/subprocess or model output is required. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is specifically "deduplication logic" and can be validated with injected clock/time values.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with configurable window and fake clock. Submit same file path twice within window and assert second request is discarded; advance clock past window and assert request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate file events (REAL-INFRA). In-process fakes can hide watcher-specific duplication/timing patterns (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration due to concrete text: "operating system emits multiple filesystem events".
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory. Perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write then rename/flush) and assert exactly one processing/output artifact for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Requires real filesystem lifecycle events (delete/recreate) and long-running watch reattachment behavior (REAL-INFRA). Mocked directory events can miss real watcher invalidation edge cases (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration by behavior text: "removed and then recreated while the daemon is running" and "without a restart".
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory. Remove the directory, recreate it at same path, then write a new file. Assert daemon detects and processes post-recreation file without daemon restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real file metadata/timestamp semantics plus watcher arrival concurrency. Integration with mocked timestamps may pass while real FS timestamp precision/ordering differs (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Unit because behavior is cross-module: watcher intake + metadata read + scheduler ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real watched directory, create several files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation times, then assert processing order matches creation timestamp order in emitted outputs/logs.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a partial-write race with real filesystem event timing and write completion detection (REAL-INFRA). In-process fakes generally cannot reproduce OS-level write/flush timing precisely (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration due to behavior text: "still being written to when the arrival event fires".
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory. Begin a slow/streamed write to a file and emit/observe arrival event timing. Assert daemon does not process intermediate partial content and only processes after write stream closes/completes.
